content:
# Trace Element Geochemistry

**Summary**: Chemical elements present in small amounts in minerals or rocks.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[1996-matter|Matter, 1996]] analyzed trace elements such as Uranium (up to 300 ppm) and Vanadium (up to 3000 ppm).

- [[2015-koeshidayatullah|Koeshidayatullah, 2015]] used trace-element-geochemistry to distinguish between different diagenetic systems in the Wadi Waqb and Ad-Dubaybah localities.

- [[2024-al-hakimi|Al-Hakimi, 2024]] used trace element geochemistry to distinguish between A-type and I-type granites.

- [[2024-sulistyohariyanto|Sulistyohariyanto, 2024]] used trace-element-geochemistry to identify REE and HFSE enrichment.

## Instruments and Software



- [[2015-koeshidayatullah|Koeshidayatullah, 2015]] used trace-element-geochemistry to distinguish between different diagenetic systems in the Wadi Waqb and Ad-Dubaybah localities.

- [[2024-al-hakimi|Al-Hakimi, 2024]] used trace element geochemistry to distinguish between A-type and I-type granites.

- [[2024-sulistyohariyanto|Sulistyohariyanto, 2024]] used trace-element-geochemistry to identify REE and HFSE enrichment.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- High values of uranium and vanadium can justify the economic exploitation of the ore. ([[1996-matter|Matter, 1996, p. 26]])

- Negative correlation between Fe/Mn and δ18O suggests influence of meteoric fluids. ([[2015-koeshidayatullah|Koeshidayatullah, 2015, p. 119]])

- A-type granites were identified as having high 10,000Ga/Al ratios (>2.5) compared to I-type (<2.5). ([[2024-al-hakimi|Al-Hakimi, 2024, p. 63]])

- All units show a pronounced tetrad effect (TE1,3 > 1.1), indicating high differentiation. ([[2024-sulistyohariyanto|Sulistyohariyanto, 2024, p. 72]])
